book_summary: Titus 1-3 has full chapter coverage with source-audited-draft restored translation and mystical companion layers. The book preserves truth according to reverence, leadership as steward-care, local polemic with anti-stereotype safeguards, grace as trainer of soundness/justice/reverence, household/slavery safeguards, mercy-based renewal, and beautiful works meeting necessary needs.
translation_notes: Titus 1 Cretan quotation and circumcision polemic require anti-stereotype and anti-Jewish-misuse safeguards.
Leadership is steward-care and character, not domination.
Titus 2 household and slavery lines must be held under grace training all people into justice and beautiful works.
Civic posture in Titus 3 is not absolute obedience to injustice; it is bounded by gentleness, good works, and mercy.
source_witness_audit_flags: Titus 1:12-14 local polemic and reception history.
